rag_rules: Fix 'named thing must be an expression with a type' by resolving root identifier references or deleting invalid clauses
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Delete AGREE clauses referencing AADL property names like Stack_Size that are not valid in AGREE scope
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions maintain type consistency and use only visible identifiers
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes
Validate that all helper functions and variables are declared before use
Avoid introducing new dependencies or identifiers not present in the original AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` with proper type declarations
Component type annexes should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths
Generated AGREE code should not reference undefined or unimported types
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to fix diagnostics by adding new architecture elements
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables
Do not reference identifiers not visible in the current AADL component scope
Do not generate expressions with mismatched types or unresolved references
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type contracts
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
Requirement Analyst Agent's role in compact requirement semantics
